text.title: llamaindex-agents-eval Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 high-impact failure modes for llamaindex-agents-eval with detectors and fixes that map back to rules in 01-core-rules.xml.

antipattern.symptom: Agent eval scores 95% but real users complain
antipattern.root-cause: Eval set leaked into training/prompt during dev.
antipattern.fix: Hold out 20% of labeled set as a 'sealed' eval used only in CI; never read it during dev.
antipattern.detector: Hash the sealed set; alert if any developer accessed the file.

antipattern.symptom: ReActAgent hangs on transient tool errors
antipattern.root-cause: ReActAgent does not auto-recover from tool exceptions by default.
antipattern.fix: Wrap every tool with try/except returning a structured error string; let the agent's reasoning loop decide whether to retry.
antipattern.detector: Force-fail one tool; verify agent surfaces a graceful response within max_turns.

antipattern.root-cause: Faithfulness + relevancy + correctness all running on full sample set with GPT-4-class judge.
antipattern.fix: Run sample_count proportional to eval_budget_usd; cap at the smallest count that gives stable variance.

antipattern.root-cause: Streaming was enabled on an agent that returns large tool-call results in chunks; downstream parser expects full output.
antipattern.fix: Disable streaming for agents whose tool outputs must be parsed whole; use streaming only for the final answer.
antipattern.detector: Diff streaming-on vs streaming-off outputs; if parser fails, streaming is wrong choice.
